hypothesis: Integrating a specialized molecular image detection and segmentation pre-processing module—trained specifically to localize and isolate chemical structure depictions within complex scientific article layouts—into the single-agent pipeline will yield statistically significant improvements in SMILES extraction F1 scores (from near-zero to above 0.40) across both the nanozymes and complexes ChemX datasets, because the current near-zero SMILES performance is attributable primarily to the absence of reliable molecular image localization rather than to the LLM's capacity to interpret correctly segmented chemical structure images.
